a cross-platform toolkit for probing networks in python

______________________________________________________________________

created by @malachi196

functions.name: hostprobe._hostprobe.check_host
documentation:
check host to see if its online! 

(good for threading: built in result queue!)

functions.name: hostprobe._hostprobe.netprobe
documentation:
netprobe: cross-platform python network scanner
-----------------------------------------------------------------
scans network for online devices (alternate version of nmap for python)

### parameters
network: the gateway ip address for scanning (don't set the subnet, or host bits)

verbose: way to much extra info 

retry: retry all offline results 

port: the port used 

maxthreads: specifies the maximum number of threads (0 or less for no max) 

info: shows percent complete and the ram usage (only for no verbose) 

r: return output list 

output: print an output list 

threshold: specify if you want to stop thread production at a certain memory limit until more space is freed 

maxthreshold: the maximum memory usage if threshold constraining is active

functions.name: hostprobe.utils.mebibyte
documentation:
value, represented in mebibytes, that is converted into its binary byte representation as an integer

`` `
\>>> one_mib = mebibyte(1)
\>>> print(one_mib)
1048576
` ``
